comment.body:
Just trying to understand the difference between what Alma and OD are saying. 
“Charm flip points are where the dealers’ exposure convexity changes both for gamma and charm. 

They often line up with a gamma trough / local low, so as price moves into the flip, gamma decays, which reduces hedging pressure and lets price travel more easily.

At/just below this point, gamma typically starts building again, so hedging pressure can re-engage in the opposite direction.

On the charm side, it’s the boundary between supportive vs. suppressive charm. With $SPX in a supportive regime, any accumulated delta decay may be hedged as we approached the flip—before charm turns suppressive.”
comment.raw_body:
Just trying to understand the difference between what Alma and OD are saying. 
“Charm flip points are where the dealers’ exposure convexity changes both for gamma and charm. 

They often line up with a gamma trough / local low, so as price moves into the flip, gamma decays, which reduces hedging pressure and lets price travel more easily.

At/just below this point, gamma typically starts building again, so hedging pressure can re-engage in the opposite direction.

On the charm side, it’s the boundary between supportive vs. suppressive charm. With $SPX in a supportive regime, any accumulated delta decay may be hedged as we approached the flip—before charm turns suppressive.”
